fps_counter.c(3) Library Functions Manual fps_counter.c(3)

NAME

fps_counter.c - FPS counter implementation using rolling-window timestamp buffer.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct fps_counter_s
FPS counter state.

Macros


#define FPS_WINDOW_SIZE 30
Number of frames to average over.

Functions


fps_counter_t * fps_counter_create (void)
void fps_counter_destroy (fps_counter_t *counter)
void fps_counter_tick (fps_counter_t *counter)
float fps_counter_get (fps_counter_t *counter)

Detailed Description

FPS counter implementation using rolling-window timestamp buffer.

Definition in file fps_counter.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define FPS_WINDOW_SIZE 30

Number of frames to average over.

Definition at line 15 of file fps_counter.c.

Referenced by fps_counter_get(), and fps_counter_tick().

Function Documentation

fps_counter_t * fps_counter_create (void )

Definition at line 33 of file fps_counter.c.

33                                         {
34   fps_counter_t *counter = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(fps_counter_t), fps_counter_t *);
35   if (!counter) {
36     return NULL;
37   }
38 
39   // Initialize circular buffer pointers
40   counter->head = 0;
41   counter->count = 0;
42   memset(counter->frame_times, 0, sizeof(counter->frame_times));
43 
44   return counter;
45 }

  fps_counter_destroy (fps_counter_t * counter)

47                                                  {
48   if (counter) {
49     SAFE_FREE(counter);
50   }

Referenced by session_display_destroy().






float
  fps_counter_get (fps_counter_t * counter)


Definition at line 74 of file fps_counter.c.


74                                               {
75   if (!counter || counter->count < 2) {
76     return 0.0f;
77   }
78 
79   // Find indices of oldest and newest timestamps in the buffer
80   int oldest_idx = (counter->head - counter->count + FPS_WINDOW_SIZE) % FPS_WINDOW_SIZE;
81   int newest_idx = (counter->head - 1 + FPS_WINDOW_SIZE) % FPS_WINDOW_SIZE;
82 
83   // Get the elapsed time between oldest and newest frames
84   uint64_t oldest_time = counter->frame_times[oldest_idx];
85   uint64_t newest_time = counter->frame_times[newest_idx];
86   uint64_t elapsed_ns = newest_time - oldest_time;
87 
88   // Avoid division by zero
89   if (elapsed_ns == 0) {
90     return 0.0f;
91   }
92 
93   // FPS = (number of frames) / elapsed_time
94   // We have (count - 1) frames across the elapsed time
95   // (count - 1) frames means count timestamps, so count-1 intervals
96   return (float)(counter->count - 1) * 1e9f / (float)elapsed_ns;
97 }






References FPS_WINDOW_SIZE.


Referenced by session_display_render_fps_overlay().






void
  fps_counter_tick (fps_counter_t * counter)


Definition at line 57 of file fps_counter.c.


57                                               {
58   if (!counter) {
59     return;
60   }
61 
62   // Record current time at head position
63   counter->frame_times[counter->head] = time_get_ns();
64 
65   // Advance head pointer with wraparound
66   counter->head = (counter->head + 1) % FPS_WINDOW_SIZE;
67 
68   // Track how many valid entries we have (up to FPS_WINDOW_SIZE)
69   if (counter->count < FPS_WINDOW_SIZE) {
70     counter->count++;
71   }
72 }






References FPS_WINDOW_SIZE, and time_get_ns().
